headlines.title: Fishermen capture the moment earthquake hits Venezuelan coast
headlines.snippet: Fishermen captured the moment twin earthquakes struck Venezuela’s La Guaira coastline on Wednesday. The tremors were felt significantly in the region.

headlines.title: US strikes on Iran test fragile ceasefire
headlines.snippet: The US has carried out strikes on Iran, marking the first such action since a Memorandum of Understanding was reached between the two nations on June 17.

headlines.title: Rescue efforts turn to recovery as aftershocks shake Venezuela
headlines.snippet: Two days after twin earthquakes hit Caracas, rescue workers report that no aid has yet arrived in one neighborhood. Recovery efforts are underway as aftershocks continue to shake Venezuela.

headlines.title: Newly released video exposes Henry Nowak killer’s lies
headlines.snippet: Newly released footage has exposed a series of lies that led UK police to arrest a stabbing victim who was dying. The video challenges the initial investigation.

headlines.title: Trump justifies strikes on Iran amid ceasefire
headlines.snippet: Trump stated that the strikes on Iran were justified, citing an Iranian attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as retaliation. This occurred amid a fragile ceasefire.

headlines.title: Video: Netanyahu says Israel will remain in southern Lebanon until Hezbollah ‘disarm’
headlines.snippet: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israel will remain in southern Lebanon until Hezbollah disarms. This statement follows a framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon, brokered by the United States.

headlines.title: Morning update
headlines.snippet: A fragile US-Iran ceasefire is under strain following US strikes on Iranian targets in response to an alleged drone attack on a commercial vessel. Iran retaliated by targeting US positions.

headlines.title: Pakistan reaffirms support for regional peace in call with Iran
headlines.snippet: Pakistan's foreign minister, Mohammad Ishaq Dar, reaffirmed Islamabad's commitment to regional peace during a call with Iran's foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi. Araghchi thanked Pakistan for its support in advancing the peace process.

headlines.title: Israel to reduce forces in southern Lebanon, army radio says
headlines.snippet: The Israeli military plans to reduce its forces in southern Lebanon and withdraw some combat brigades, according to Israeli Army Radio. This decision comes after a framework agreement was reached between Lebanon and Israel with US involvement.

headlines.title: Video: Protesters rally in Beirut against Israel-Lebanon agreement
headlines.snippet: Protesters gathered in Beirut to demonstrate against the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement signed in Washington. Hezbollah has voiced opposition to the deal.

headlines.title: Russian drone strikes kill two in Ukraine’s Dnipropetrovsk region, one in Sumy region
headlines.snippet: Russian drone strikes resulted in the deaths of two people in Ukraine’s Dnipropetrovsk region and one in the Sumy region.

headlines.title: Policemen Rescue Toddler From Destroyed Building in La Guaira
headlines.snippet: Policemen successfully rescued a toddler from a destroyed building in La Guaira, Venezuela.

headlines.title: Fragments of Aircraft Fall After Purportedly Hitting Beijing Skyscraper
headlines.snippet: Fragments of an aircraft have fallen after reportedly striking a skyscraper in Beijing.

headlines.title: Lebanon, Israel, US sign trilateral framework agreement in Washington
headlines.snippet: Lebanon, Israel, and the United States have signed a trilateral framework agreement in Washington.

headlines.title: Trump slams Iran drone attack as ‘foolish’ ceasefire violation
headlines.snippet: Trump described the Iranian drone attack as a 'foolish' violation of the ceasefire.

headlines.title: US strikes Iran sites after cargo ship attack, Tehran retaliates
headlines.snippet: US strikes on Iranian military sites followed an attack on a cargo ship, raising concerns about security in the Strait of Hormuz and threatening a fragile ceasefire.

headlines.title: France’s terrorism apology law faces test in pro-Palestine case
headlines.snippet: A Paris court has frozen a trial related to France's terrorism apology law, raising a constitutional question about civil party powers. Campaigners view this as a victory for the Palestine movement.

headlines.title: Ms Rachel says world has failed Palestinian children at briefing
headlines.snippet: Ms Rachel expressed emotion during a briefing on Palestinian children, apologizing to a young girl and urging world leaders to take action.

headlines.title: Shia Muslims mark their holiest day in war-battered Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Shia Muslims observed Ashura by marching through the ruins of Nabatieh, defying the destruction from Israel's war on south Lebanon.

headlines.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon-Israel deal, warns of civil war
headlines.snippet: Hezbollah has rejected the Lebanon-Israel framework agreement, warning that its implementation could lead to civil war. Netanyahu stated that troops would remain in Lebanon.

headlines.snippet: The primary achievement of the agreement between the United States and Iran is the cessation of open conflict, potentially saving lives after the memorandum of understanding was signed to halt fighting.

headlines.snippet: Saudi authorities have suspended travel and entry from the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda, and South Sudan due to concerns about the potential spread of the Ebola virus.
